message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The current USA 2021 CoVID-19 Winter Resurgence is modeled here with the same function used for analyzing prior USA CoVID-19 waves:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn1">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_ueqn1.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                  Here,
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  (
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  ) gives the total number of CoVID-19 cases above the previous baseline, and
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  sets the initial
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>dbl</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  =
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  (ln 2) pandemic
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>dbl</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  doubling time. Larger
                  <jats:italic>α</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  values indicate that uninfected people are improving their pandemic mitigation efforts, such as
                  <jats:italic>Social Distancing</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>vaccinations</jats:italic>
                  ; while
                  <jats:italic>δ</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>0</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  <jats:italic>></jats:italic>
                  0 accelerates the post-peak
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_inline1.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  <jats:italic>tail-off</jats:italic>
                  , and is empirically associated with
                  <jats:italic>mask-wearing</jats:italic>
                  . The pandemic wave end is when
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  (
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  ) no longer increases.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The USA Summer 2021 resurgence results from our prior
                  <jats:italic>medrxiv</jats:italic>
                  .
                  <jats:italic>org</jats:italic>
                  preprints
                  <jats:sup>*</jats:sup>
                  were used as a baseline. By 11/15/2021, an additional
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_inline2.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  cases above baseline were found, signaling the USA Winter 2021 resurgence. This CoVID-19 wave is still in its
                  <jats:italic>initial</jats:italic>
                  stages. Presently, our analysis indicates that this CoVID-19 wave can infect virtually all susceptible persons; just like the
                  <jats:italic>initial</jats:italic>
                  stage of the USA Summer 2021 resurgence. Data up through 12/30/2021 gives these paremeter values:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn2">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_ueqn2.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                  These values are identical to the prior 2020 USA Winter Resurgence results. Also, the
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_inline3.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  and the
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_inline4.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  values are similar. However, while the Winter 2020 Resurgence showed a significant
                  <jats:italic>mask-wearing</jats:italic>
                  effect
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_inline5.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  , this
                  <jats:italic>initial</jats:italic>
                  USA Winter 2021 Resurgence shows practically no
                  <jats:italic>mask-wearing</jats:italic>
                  effects
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_inline6.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  . If
                  <jats:italic>mask-wearing</jats:italic>
                  were to quickly rise to the Winter 2020 levels, it would give these projected totals:
                  <jats:disp-formula id="ueqn3">
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22268868v1_ueqn3.gif" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:disp-formula>
                  More robust
                  <jats:italic>mask-wearing</jats:italic>
                  and enhanced
                  <jats:italic>Social Distancing</jats:italic>
                  measures could further reduce these values (
                  <jats:italic>with 3 Figures</jats:italic>
                  ).
                </jats:p>
title: Modeling the USA Winter 2021 CoVID-19 Resurgence

reference.unstructured: https://medrxiv.org/cgi/content/short/2020.05.04.20091207v1 “Initial Model for the Impact of Social Distancing on CoVID-19 Spread”
https://medrxiv.org/cgi/content/short/2020.06.30.20143149v1 “Orthogonal Functions for Evaluating Social Distancing Impact on CoVID-19 Spread”
https://medrxiv.org/cgi/content/short/2020.08.07.20169904 “Model to Describe Fast Shutoff of CoVID-19 Pandemic Spread”
https://medrxiv.org/cgi/content/short/2021.08.16.21262150 “The IHME vs Me: Modeling USA CoVID-19 Spread, Early Data to the Fifth Wave”
https://medrxiv.org/cgi/content/short/2021.10.15.21265078 “Updated Model for the USA Summer 2021 CoVID-19 Resurgence”
